steps.title: Compare to circle form
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation:
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Identify the center and radius
steps.content:
Compare the given equation to standard form:
$$(x-11)^2+(y-n)^2=49$$
The center is $(11,n)$, and the radius is:
$$\sqrt{49}=7$$

steps.title: Match the description
steps.content: Choice A gives center $(11,n)$ and radius $7$. The answer is A.
